jokes.text: Rocky IV is the greatest movie of all time vs Million Dollar Baby won an Oscar
jokes.setup: Mac declares Rocky IV the greatest movie ever
jokes.punchline: Dee counters with Million Dollar Baby because it won an Oscar
jokes.explanation: Sets up character dynamics - Mac's lowbrow taste vs Dee's pretentiousness. Functional opener.

jokes.text: Girls can't fight. They don't have muscles. It's not sexist. It's just truthful.
jokes.punchline: Mac doubles down claiming his sexism is just 'truthful'
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Mac's complete lack of self-awareness about his obvious prejudices.

jokes.text: Girls can't pull trucks through snow. Can you pull trucks through snow? I absolutely could.
jokes.setup: Mac claims women can't pull trucks
jokes.punchline: Dee confidently claims she absolutely could
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with Dee's delusional confidence. The immediate certainty is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: You can barely walk in the snow
jokes.setup: Dee claims she can pull trucks
jokes.punchline: Mac points out she can barely walk in snow
jokes.explanation: Good deflating comeback that undercuts Dee's bravado efficiently.

jokes.text: Time out. Is the truck parked in neutral?
jokes.punchline: Charlie interrupts with practical logistics question
jokes.explanation: Brilliant interruption that deflates the argument by treating the absurd premise seriously. Perfect Charlie logic.

jokes.text: Empty your pockets. Now. Hey, let's just all stay calm.
jokes.punchline: Dennis tries to de-escalate with politeness
jokes.explanation: Dennis's inappropriate politeness during a mugging is character-appropriate cringe.

jokes.text: Nice friends.
jokes.setup: The gang presumably abandoned Dee during mugging
jokes.punchline: Mugger sarcastically comments on their loyalty
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from an unexpected source - the mugger being the moral compass is perfectly Sunny.

jokes.text: And a Ford F-150. Right. In park, but no emergency brake. Maybe I could pull it four feet.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific parameters for a ridiculous scenario
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the absurdist premise with Charlie's methodical approach to nonsense.

jokes.text: Just got back from a 'Boxercise' class at the gym. 'Boxercise'? What kind of queer shit is that?
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately mocks it as 'queer shit'
jokes.explanation: Mac's immediate homophobic reaction is character-appropriate but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: All during the '60s, before I met my whore wife, I was a boxer. They called me Frankie Fast Hands.
jokes.punchline: Casually calls his wife a whore while bragging about boxing
jokes.explanation: Frank's casual misogyny while establishing his boxing credentials is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Keep livin' in the past, Dad.
jokes.setup: Frank boasts about his boxing nickname
jokes.punchline: Mac dismissively tells him to keep living in the past
jokes.explanation: Mac's dismissal of Frank's glory days is a solid deflating moment.

jokes.text: You ditched your sister? Absolutely. With a mugger... The guy had a knife. Of course we did. What were we supposed to do?
jokes.setup: Frank asks if they abandoned Dee
jokes.punchline: They matter-of-factly confirm they ditched her with a knife-wielding mugger
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing their complete lack of loyalty or basic human decency.

jokes.text: Ain't no rules out on the streets. You guys got no rules on the streets, you.
jokes.setup: Frank and Dennis discussing street fighting
jokes.punchline: Dennis mocking Frank's attempt to sound street-wise
jokes.explanation: Dennis calling out Frank's posturing is good character interplay.

jokes.text: Will you stop talking about the streets you three jackasses, like you've been there?
jokes.setup: The guys posturing about street fighting
jokes.punchline: Dee calls out their obvious lack of street experience
jokes.explanation: Dee being the voice of reason while insulting them is solid character work.

jokes.text: There's blood everywhere. It's beautiful. These guys don't have technique. They get all cranked up like animals.
jokes.punchline: They find the violence beautiful and animalistic
jokes.explanation: Their appreciation for violence while missing the point is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You know who can take a really good beating? Charlie. Charlie.
jokes.setup: Looking for someone who can take punishment
jokes.punchline: They immediately think of Charlie as someone who can be beaten
jokes.explanation: Dark and efficient - their immediate agreement about Charlie's abuse tolerance is perfectly callous.

jokes.text: That's the smell of big dreams. The smell of glory. It smells like feet. And feet. Does smell like feet.
jokes.punchline: Even Frank has to admit it smells like feet
jokes.explanation: Good follow-through on the feet joke with Frank's reluctant admission.

jokes.text: Frankie Fast Hands! Holy crap. You... really let yourself go.
jokes.setup: Bobby recognizes Frank from his boxing days
jokes.punchline: Immediately insults Frank's current appearance
jokes.explanation: Standard insult about aging, but the immediate shift from recognition to insult works.

jokes.text: Amazing. Hey, she's pretty too, huh? Like her old man, huh?
jokes.punchline: Implies he's attractive while clearly not being so
jokes.explanation: Bobby's delusional self-regard is a decent character moment.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, that's an annoying sound he makes. Ass... hole. Yeah, asshole.
jokes.punchline: Frank and Dee bonding over calling him an asshole
jokes.explanation: Nice father-daughter bonding moment over shared hatred.

jokes.text: Here's what you're gonna do. You're gonna take all the weight on your neck. Then you're gonna jam your legs down and hyperextend your ankles and then shoot back up and lock your knees in place.
jokes.punchline: Gives completely dangerous and wrong instruction
jokes.explanation: Frank's confident delivery of terrible advice that could cause serious injury is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Not one of those things sounds right to me at all.
jokes.punchline: Dee's reasonable but understated concern
jokes.explanation: Great understatement - 'not one of those things sounds right' for advice that could paralyze her.

jokes.text: You wanna fight like a man? You gotta train like a man. Daddy, I can't. It's... I can't.
jokes.setup: Frank forcing Dee to do dangerous exercise
jokes.punchline: Dee's obvious distress being ignored
jokes.explanation: Frank's abusive training methods create uncomfortable cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Charlie, you're, like, the toughest guy we know. Yeah? So nobody can kick your ass, right? Practically invincible.
jokes.punchline: Setting him up to be a punching bag
jokes.explanation: Good manipulation setup showing how they exploit Charlie's need for validation.

jokes.text: Now it's becoming this whole big thing. It feels a little gay. It's not gay. Just take your shirt off. It's not gay.
jokes.setup: Charlie resisting taking his shirt off
jokes.punchline: The guys' insistence making it more suspicious
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where their protests make it seem more gay, not less.

jokes.text: Why can't I just get, like, all excited and passionately rip it off in the moment?
jokes.setup: Charlie wanting to take shirt off dramatically
jokes.punchline: His romantic idea of shirt removal in context
jokes.explanation: Charlie's weird romanticism about shirt removal is perfectly absurd and quotable.

jokes.text: What's that made out of? It's a board, so it's, you know, made out of wood. It's, like, particle board. It's, like, harder than wood, dude. No. It's actually softer than wood.
jokes.setup: Charlie hurt his hand punching board
jokes.punchline: Confused debate about board hardness
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist confusion about basic material properties while Charlie is in pain.

jokes.text: I wasn't drunk enough. You should be drunk for this. We should all be drunk for this.
jokes.punchline: Solution is more alcohol for everyone
jokes.explanation: Their solution to everything being alcohol is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Just grab that bar and jerk it down behind your neck.
jokes.punchline: More terrible exercise advice that could cause injury
jokes.explanation: Callback to Frank's consistently dangerous training methods.

jokes.text: They billed it as 'Beauty Versus the Beast.' Wasn't much of a fight. First-round knockout.
jokes.setup: Bobby describing old fight with Frank
jokes.punchline: Implies Frank was 'the Beast' and got knocked out
jokes.explanation: Good setup and payoff about Frank's supposed boxing prowess being false.

jokes.text: You know damn well you hit me after the bell. That was a cheap shot. Excuses are like assholes. Everybody's got one, and they all stink.
jokes.punchline: Bobby's crude dismissal with asshole metaphor
jokes.explanation: Standard crude saying - not particularly original or clever.

jokes.text: I let my daughter do my fighting for me. And maybe in a couple years, when this little graham cracker might have the stuff to compete, maybe we'll see.
jokes.punchline: Calls Dee a 'little graham cracker'
jokes.explanation: 'Little graham cracker' is a memorable insult that captures Dee's fragility.

jokes.text: Listen, little buttercup, I'm just bustin' your old man's balls. You don't have to get your panties in an uproar.
jokes.punchline: Bobby's condescending dismissal with sexist language
jokes.explanation: Standard sexist dismissal - functional but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: If only there was a way that you could be physically tough enough to intimidate men. Let's just say I provide a much-needed service around this place. Step into the shadows and, uh, we'll talk.
jokes.punchline: Obvious drug dealer setup with 'step into the shadows'
jokes.explanation: Standard drug dealer introduction but sets up Dee's steroid use effectively.

jokes.text: What? It's like 11:00 in the morning here. We don't care. Open the door. Surprise, bitch.
jokes.setup: Mac and Dennis demanding entry to Charlie's apartment
jokes.punchline: Charlie noting inappropriate timing, Dennis saying 'Surprise, bitch'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing their disregard for Charlie's schedule and boundaries.

jokes.text: Hey! Guys! I can handle a chair. Go get a chair. See what you're doing to him? You're getting him all worked up.
jokes.setup: Debate about whether Charlie can handle chair
jokes.punchline: Charlie eager for more abuse, Mac noting his excitement
jokes.explanation: Charlie's enthusiasm for being abused and Mac's casual observation is darkly funny.

jokes.text: No, no, no, no. Of course not. These are performance-enhancing supplements. You ever punched a guy so hard in the eye he swallowed it?
jokes.punchline: Describes impossibly violent side effect
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation - punching someone so hard they swallow their own eye is memorably ridiculous.

jokes.text: Every time I'm gonna take... Why can't you just take it off? Just take it off. You're ruining the moment.
jokes.setup: Still trying to get Charlie's shirt off
jokes.punchline: Charlie's frustration about 'ruining the moment'
jokes.explanation: Great callback to Charlie's romantic ideas about shirt removal, getting more absurd.

jokes.text: Just 10 more minutes. / No, no. That's it. Dee, that's... / I love it. I need more of it.
jokes.setup: Frank trying to end Dee's training session
jokes.punchline: Dee's obvious steroid addiction making her desperate for more training
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Dee's growing steroid dependency

jokes.text: You look like a Holocaust victim in pageant makeup. I will eat your babies, bitch.
jokes.punchline: Dee's shocking insult followed by threat to eat babies
jokes.explanation: Shockingly dark and specific insult that shows how far gone Dee is on drugs. Memorable for its extremeness.

jokes.text: What's the matter, 'Frankie Fat Hands'? You're afraid she's gonna get knocked out just like you did?
jokes.punchline: Changes 'Fast Hands' to 'Fat Hands' and references past defeat
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay callback that turns Frank's old nickname into an insult.

jokes.text: Why don't you shut your fat little monkey face and hold the bag?
jokes.punchline: Extreme insult toward her own father
jokes.explanation: Shows how drugs have made Dee completely hostile, even to family. The specificity of 'fat little monkey face' is memorable.

jokes.text: This one might as well be goddamn camouflaged! Oh, good. Are you happy now? I just punched a hole in my wall!
jokes.punchline: Roid rage causes her to punch wall
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing the extreme effects of steroid abuse on Dee's behavior.

jokes.text: Clown Baby. Clown Ba... Clown Baby? Yeah. You mean like a baby clown? Not a baby clown. A clown ba... It's like a baby... It's like a clown that is a baby.
jokes.punchline: Confused explanation of 'Clown Baby' concept
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist name that becomes more confusing when explained. The distinction between 'baby clown' and 'clown baby' is perfectly nonsensical.

jokes.text: Nobody's gonna bet on a guy named Clown Baby. He's gonna be a huge underdog. Which means we stand to make even more money off him.
jokes.punchline: Their scheme logic about betting odds
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing their scheming mindset and how they see Charlie as a commodity.

jokes.text: I might have had some. What did you just say, you little bitch? I might have had some of your pills or whatever.
jokes.punchline: Charlie casually admits to taking them
jokes.explanation: Charlie's casual admission while Dee rages sets up their mutual drug-fueled violence.

jokes.text: I might have had some of your pills or whatever. / Oh, yeah? / I am gonna punch a hole through your face.
jokes.setup: Charlie casually admitting to taking Dee's steroids
jokes.punchline: Dee's immediate violent response to the casual admission
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from casual admission to violent threat, both characters on steroids

jokes.text: I am gonna punch a hole through your face. I'd like to see you try that. I am gonna rip your face off... Make a shirt out of it, and I'm gonna wear it.
jokes.punchline: Charlie's specific plan to make a face shirt
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and disturbing threat that's memorable for its creativity and darkness. Pure Charlie logic on drugs.

jokes.text: I'm gonna take one second to take my shirt off, and then you're gonna die!
jokes.punchline: Finally willing to dramatically remove shirt for violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the running gag - Charlie finally gets his dramatic shirt removal moment, but for violence instead of training.

jokes.text: He's in jail with Dee. / What? / Apparently they got all hopped up on amphetamines... and beat the shit out of some guy in front of the bar.
jokes.setup: Looking for Charlie for the fight
jokes.punchline: He's in jail for steroid-fueled violence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - their drug use led to actual criminal consequences

jokes.text: Apparently they got all hopped up on amphetamines and beat the shit out of some guy in front of the bar.
jokes.setup: Explaining where Charlie and Dee are
jokes.punchline: Casual description of their drug-fueled violence
jokes.explanation: Mac's matter-of-fact delivery of shocking news is typical character work.

jokes.text: I had to change the bet... a little. / You bet against me? / I bet against you.
jokes.setup: Mac asking about Dennis's mysterious money
jokes.punchline: Dennis casually admitting betrayal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis betrayal - casual admission of betting against his friend

jokes.text: Where did you get that money? I had to change the bet... a little. You bet against me? I bet against you.
jokes.setup: Mac discovering Dennis has money after losing
jokes.punchline: Dennis casually admits to betting against his friend
jokes.explanation: Perfect character betrayal moment with Dennis's casual admission of his scheme against Mac.

jokes.text: I could have taken a dive. I wouldn't have had to get my ass kicked.
jokes.setup: Mac realizing Dennis could have told him
jokes.punchline: Mac would have happily thrown the fight if he'd known
jokes.explanation: Great Mac logic - he's not mad about the betrayal, just the unnecessary beating

jokes.text: I could have taken a dive. I wouldn't have had to get my ass kicked. Mac, it had to look real.
jokes.punchline: Dennis explaining his manipulation like a movie
jokes.explanation: Dennis's cold calculation about Mac's suffering is perfect character work.

jokes.text: You know what? Stab this son of a bitch. Oh, wait a second. Oh, come on, Mac. Don't be like that, man.
jokes.punchline: Tells mugger to stab Dennis, who immediately backpedals
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with Mac's revenge and Dennis's immediate panic. Dark but perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Holy shit!
jokes.setup: Frank and Bobby about to fight with 'ground rules'
jokes.punchline: Presumably Frank immediately sucker punches Bobby
jokes.explanation: Visual punchline where Frank presumably doesn't wait for rules and immediately attacks. Perfect character moment.
